item.title: Applied Physics Research
item.description:
These past five months, I have been working in The Billinge Group. Broadly, I have been contributing to solving the nanostructure problem, through maintaining and deploying packages (old and new) within our Atomic Structure Analysis Software, Diffpy.

  • Lead the deployment and maintenance pdffit2 and pdfgui.
  • Documenting and formalizing a streamlined software deployment process for all current & future packages within the group with Andrew Yang.
  • Collaborating with Ibrhaim Adnan on designing novel CV algorithms for classifying x-ray diffraction data here. (our repo will soon be public)

item.title: RL Trading Model
item.description:
Under Construction.

Over the summer I got work as a data science intern for a crypto hedge fund, Floating Point Group.

  • ADD

item.title: Optimal way to play Blackjack
item.description:
This was an independent study conducted under Farez Siddiqui, through the Math Department’s Directed Reading Program (DRP).

  • Researched reinforment learning with an emphasis on applications in finance. My primary research text was Barto and Sutton’s Reinforcement Learning.
  • Designed an RL model using monte carlo simulations to find the optimal policy for playing blackjack. You can find it on my github, here.
  • Presented my project at FSU’s Undergraduate Research Symposium.
